item.title: EVA: Efficient Reinforcement Learning for End-to-End Video Agent
item.description: Video understanding with multimodal large language models (MLLMs) remains challenging due to the long token sequences of videos, which contain extensive temporal dependencies and redundant frames. Existing approaches typically treat MLLM...

item.title: CarePilot: A Multi-Agent Framework for Long-Horizon Computer Task Automation in Healthcare
item.description: Multimodal agentic pipelines are transforming human-computer interaction by enabling efficient and accessible automation of complex, real-world tasks. However, recent efforts have focused on short-horizon or general-purpose applications...

item.title: CUA-Suite: Massive Human-annotated Video Demonstrations for Computer-Use Agents
item.description: Computer-use agents (CUAs) hold great promise for automating complex desktop workflows, yet progress toward general-purpose agents is bottlenecked by the scarcity of continuous, high-quality human demonstration videos. Recent work emphas...

item.title: UI-Voyager: A Self-Evolving GUI Agent Learning via Failed Experience
item.description: Autonomous mobile GUI agents have attracted increasing attention along with the advancement of Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs). However, existing methods still suffer from inefficient learning from failed trajectories and ambigu...

item.title: T-MAP: Red-Teaming LLM Agents with Trajectory-aware Evolutionary Search
item.description: While prior red-teaming efforts have focused on eliciting harmful text outputs from large language models (LLMs), such approaches fail to capture agent-specific vulnerabilities that emerge through multi-step tool execution, particularly...

item.title: Understanding the Challenges in Iterative Generative Optimization with LLMs
item.description: Generative optimization uses large language models (LLMs) to iteratively improve artifacts (such as code, workflows or prompts) using execution feedback. It is a promising approach to building self-improving agents, yet in practice remai...

item.title: When Models Judge Themselves: Unsupervised Self-Evolution for Multimodal Reasoning
item.description: Recent progress in multimodal large language models has led to strong performance on reasoning tasks, but these improvements largely rely on high-quality annotated data or teacher-model distillation, both of which are costly and difficul...

item.title: OmniWeaving: Towards Unified Video Generation with Free-form Composition and Reasoning
item.description: While proprietary systems such as Seedance-2.0 have achieved remarkable success in omni-capable video generation, open-source alternatives significantly lag behind. Most academic models remain heavily fragmented, and the few existing eff...

item.title: GameplayQA: A Benchmarking Framework for Decision-Dense POV-Synced Multi-Video Understanding of 3D Virtual Agents
item.description: Multimodal LLMs are increasingly deployed as perceptual backbones for autonomous agents in 3D environments, from robotics to virtual worlds. These applications require agents to perceive rapid state changes, attribute actions to the corr...

item.title: The Pulse of Motion: Measuring Physical Frame Rate from Visual Dynamics
item.description: While recent generative video models have achieved remarkable visual realism and are being explored as world models, true physical simulation requires mastering both space and time. Current models can produce visually smooth kinematics,...

item.title: Can LLM Agents Be CFOs? A Benchmark for Resource Allocation in Dynamic Enterprise Environments
item.description: Large language models (LLMs) have enabled agentic systems that can reason, plan, and act across complex tasks, but it remains unclear whether they can allocate resources effectively under uncertainty. Unlike short-horizon reactive decisi...

item.title: UniFunc3D: Unified Active Spatial-Temporal Grounding for 3D Functionality Segmentation
item.description: Functionality segmentation in 3D scenes requires an agent to ground implicit natural-language instructions into precise masks of fine-grained interactive elements. Existing methods rely on fragmented pipelines that suffer from visual bli...

item.title: 4DGS360: 360° Gaussian Reconstruction of Dynamic Objects from a Single Video
item.description: We introduce 4DGS360, a diffusion-free framework for 360^{circ} dynamic object reconstruction from casual monocular video. Existing methods often fail to reconstruct consistent 360^{circ} geometry, as their heavy reliance on 2D-native pr...

item.title: LagerNVS: Latent Geometry for Fully Neural Real-time Novel View Synthesis
item.description: Recent work has shown that neural networks can perform 3D tasks such as Novel View Synthesis (NVS) without explicit 3D reconstruction. Even so, we argue that strong 3D inductive biases are still helpful in the design of such networks. We...

item.title: Qworld: Question-Specific Evaluation Criteria for LLMs
item.description: Evaluating large language models (LLMs) on open-ended questions is difficult because response quality depends on the question's context. Binary scores and static rubrics fail to capture these context-dependent requirements. Existing meth...

item.title: StreamingClaw Technical Report
item.description: Applications such as embodied intelligence rely on a real-time perception-decision-action closed loop, posing stringent challenges for streaming video understanding. However, current agents suffer from fragmented capabilities, such as su...

item.title: 6Bit-Diffusion: Inference-Time Mixed-Precision Quantization for Video Diffusion Models
item.description: Diffusion transformers have demonstrated remarkable capabilities in generating videos. However, their practical deployment is severely constrained by high memory usage and computational cost. Post-Training Quantization provides a practic...

item.title: Unleashing Spatial Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models via Textual Representation Guided Reasoning
item.description: Existing Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) struggle with 3D spatial reasoning, as they fail to construct structured abstractions of the 3D environment depicted in video inputs. To bridge this gap, drawing inspiration from cognitiv...

item.title: PLDR-LLMs Reason At Self-Organized Criticality
item.description: We show that PLDR-LLMs pretrained at self-organized criticality exhibit reasoning at inference time. The characteristics of PLDR-LLM deductive outputs at criticality is similar to second-order phase transitions. At criticality, the corre...

item.title: Why Does Self-Distillation (Sometimes) Degrade the Reasoning Capability of LLMs?
item.description: Self-distillation has emerged as an effective post-training paradigm for LLMs, often improving performance while shortening reasoning traces. However, in mathematical reasoning, we find that it can reduce response length while degrading...

item.title: Toward Physically Consistent Driving Video World Models under Challenging Trajectories
item.description: Video generation models have shown strong potential as world models for autonomous driving simulation. However, existing approaches are primarily trained on real-world driving datasets, which mostly contain natural and safe driving scena...

item.title: SpectralSplats: Robust Differentiable Tracking via Spectral Moment Supervision
item.description: 3D Gaussian Splatting (3DGS) enables real-time, photorealistic novel view synthesis, making it a highly attractive representation for model-based video tracking. However, leveraging the differentiability of the 3DGS renderer "in the wild...
